'''Kevin Penny''' is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], on loan for [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]<ref name="Kevin Penny signs month-long loan deal with Harlequins RL">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/wolves/wolvesnews/7988071.Penny_signs_month_long_loan_deal_with_Harlequins_RL|title=Kevin Penny signs month-long loan deal with Harlequins RL|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=7 April 2010|accessdate=7 April 2010|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110723033203/http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/wolves/wolvesnews/7988071.Penny_signs_month_long_loan_deal_with_Harlequins_RL|archivedate=23 July 2011}}</ref> and for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in rugby league's [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early career==
Born in [[Warrington]], Penny attended [[Padgate High School]] and [[Priestley College]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Back to school for Wolves players |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/4176961.back-to-school-for-wolves-players/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=6 September 2023 |date=5 March 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=There is no substitute for pace and Penny has it in abundance |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/1465271.there-is-no-substitute-for-pace-and-penny-has-it-in-abundance/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=6 September 2023 |date=13 June 2007}}</ref> He joined [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s academy in 2005 after being spotted playing rugby at school.<ref name="Penny's New Deal">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/07/19/RUGBYL_Warrington_Penny.html|title=Penny's New Deal|publisher=Sportinglife|date=19 July 2007|accessdate=19 July 2007|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930043734/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/07/19/RUGBYL_Warrington_Penny.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Club career==
[[Image:Kevin Penny.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Penny during his first spell with Warrington]]

===Warrington Wolves===
Kevin made his senior début For Warrington against the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on 1 June 2007, scoring two tries in a 42 - 26 defeat.<ref name="Leeds 42 - Warrington 26 ">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=2760800|title=Leeds 42 - Warrington 26|publisher=Super League|date=1 June 2007|accessdate=9 July 2009|archive-date=9 January 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110109054304/http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=2760800|url-status=dead}}</ref> Shortly after his début he received the engage [[Super League]] player of the month award for his efforts during the month of July 2007, despite starting the month with only 5 first grade games next to his name. He scored 5 tries in just 3 games during the month, 3 of which were scored in the space of just 9 minutes against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref name="Teenager Penny makes Dream Team ">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/6999523.stmSTORY_NAME=rleague/07/09/17/RUGBYL_Warrington_Penny.html|title=Teenager Penny makes Dream Team|publisher=BBC Sport|date=17 September 2007|accessdate=17 September 2007}}{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> Penny ended 2007 with 15 tries in 15 games and was named in the 2007 [[Super League Dream Team]]

Kevin was given the number 5 jersey for 2008 after a strong début season and played outside Australian international [[Matt King (rugby player)|Matt King]], but failed to impress and found himself out of the team.

On 26 August 2009, Kevin competed for the title of ''Fastest Man in Rugby League'' as part of a curtain raising competition at the [[Carnegie Floodlit Nines]].<ref name="Penny eyes speed crown">{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/8219860.stm|title=Holmes tips Donald to be fastest|date=25 August 2009}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
On 21 May 2009 Kevin was loaned out to Championship side [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] after failing to force his way into Warrington's first team squad under new coach [[Tony Smith (rugby league b. 1967)|Tony Smith]].<ref name="Wolves duo join Widnes on loan">{{cite web|url=http://sport.aol.co.uk/rugby-league/wolves-duo-join-widnes-on-loan/article/2009051910593785178152|title=Wolves duo join Widnes on loan|publisher=AOL|date=21 May 2009|accessdate=24 June 2009}} {{Dead link|date=October 2010|bot=H3llBot}}</ref>
[[Image:Kevin Penny Quins.JPG|thumb|right|100px|Penny in action for Harlequins RL]]

===Harlequins Rugby League===
In 2010, Penny was loaned to [[London Broncos|Harlequins RL]], scoring three tries in five matches before returning to his parent club.

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
Penny was released by Warrington at the end of 2010 and joined [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] for 2011, but once again failed to make an impression and was loaned out to Widnes Vikings.

===Swinton Lions===
Kevin Penny joined [[Swinton Lions]] when Lions coach [[Steve McCormack]] signed him in November 2011 during the club's close-season player recruitment drive for the 2012 season.

===Return to Warrington===
Penny re-signed with Warrington in 2014 and made his second début for the club against [[Castleford Tigers]]. Prior to his return Penny said he was determined to make the most out of his second chance and was aiming to give the Wolves 'value'.<ref name="Penny aims to give Wolves value">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30023/penny-aims-to-give-wolves|title=Penny aims to give Wolves value|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|url-status=bot: unknown|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140424095657/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30023/penny-aims-to-give-wolves|archivedate=24 April 2014|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Despite spending some time on loan at Swinton and [[North Wales Crusaders]], Penny displayed good form for Warrington and established himself in the club's first team. He played 23 matches and scored 16 tries in 2015, his best return since his début season, and signed a new two-year deal at the end of the year.<ref name="Warrington: Kevin Penny stays and Benjamin Jullien signs">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34338839|title=Warrington: Kevin Penny stays and Benjamin Jullien signs|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=31 December 2015|access-date=1 January 2016}}</ref>

===Swinton===
Penny spent time on loan at Swinton in 2014.

===North Wales Crusaders===
Penny spent time on loan at the North Wales Crusaders in 2015.

===Rochdale Hornets===
Penny spent time on loan at the Rochdale Hornets in 2016 and 2017.
